item.title: About
item.description: The garden rage.garden is a personal blog about software development, engineering leadership, and the organizational patterns that make both better or worse. It’s opinionated, occasionally absurd, and trying to be useful. Not a tutorial site. Not a newsletter. Not career advice. Just someone thinking out loud about the work. Why not Medium, Substack, Ghost, or whatever’s next Because I’ve watched enough platforms pivot, paywire, get acquired, or simply decide that my content is now their content.

item.title: 10 Reasons Your AI Transformation Will Fail
item.description: I survived the dot-com era, where every business plan acquired a “.com” the way ships acquire barnacles: automatically, without anyone deciding it was a good idea. I have survived, and occasionally not survived, more re-orgs than I can count, which is either a badge of honor or a warning sign. I watched entire engineering organizations migrate to the cloud because AWS existed. Each time, the pattern was the same. The press release drops, the board asks a question, a competitor makes an announcement, and the organization attempts to move.
